Context

The problem with the current 3PL model in KSA

HungerStation's 3PL model creates operational blind spots that cost money, create risk, and leave every stakeholder working without a shared system of record.

Current flow — no digital backbone
HungerStation
Sells gear to 3PLs
→
~700 3PLs
Buy gear, distribute manually
→
Riders
Receive gear via WhatsApp/paper
Compliance (Captains)
Checks via Boolanga app ✓
↛
Gear lifecycle data
MISSING — no connection
🏢

HungerStation's blind spots

  • No visibility into whether 3PLs actually distribute gear to riders
  • No data on gear lifecycle — nobody tracks expiry or replacement timelines
  • Deduction disputes between HS and 3PLs are resolved with spreadsheets
  • Compliance checks happen but are disconnected from gear records
  • Cannot score or rank 3PLs based on operational performance
🚚

3PL pain points

  • No proof of gear distribution — when a rider says "I never got it", the 3PL has no defense
  • Deduction overcharges from HS with no audit trail to dispute
  • Rider clearance is manual — gear returns tracked in notebooks
  • No way to demonstrate compliance to HS or regulators
  • Each 3PL reinvents the wheel with 20-50 riders
The solution

One platform. Three products. Every stakeholder connected.

The 3PL Operations License gives every partner the exact same digital tools that power Talabat's rider gear operations across 8 MENA markets — proven at scale.

New flow — Boolanga as the digital backbone

Compliance App 2.0 — the full upgrade, delivered free

HungerStation previously requested these enhancements to the existing compliance solution. As part of the 3PL Operations License partnership, Boolanga will deliver every item below at no additional cost to HungerStation.

12
New modules
FREE
For HungerStation
2.0
Faster, optimized apps

1Clock In / Clock Out

Captains clock in and out with compliance verification built into the shift start.

  • Time and date stamp on every shift
  • Selfie verification at clock-in
  • Vehicle photo capture (cleanliness and compliance check)

2Rider Information Search

Find any rider instantly — two search methods for field flexibility.

  • Search by Rider ID
  • Search by Plate Number (synced via Rooster per rider)

3Configurable Questionnaires

Fully dynamic, logic-driven questionnaires that adapt to your compliance needs.

  • Dynamic question flows with conditional logic
  • Multiple question types (multiple choice, text, photo, rating)
  • Configurable from the backend without code changes

4Equipment Management View

During field checks, Captains see the rider's equipment record in real time.

  • Equipment issued to each rider (synced via Rooster)
  • Size, type, and lifecycle status per item
  • Flag expired or missing gear on the spot

5Accident Reporting

Structured accident reporting with media attachments and automatic Captain assignment.

  • Structured accident form with required fields
  • Photo and video attachments
  • Automatic routing to closest Captain

6Tutorial Content Section

Training materials available directly inside the Captain app — no switching between tools.

  • Upload video, PDF, and text training materials
  • Categorization and search functionality
  • Accessible from the Captain app at any time

7Incident Reporting

Report general incidents without needing a Rider ID or Plate Number — a dedicated button in the Captain app.

  • Simple static form for general incidents
  • Descriptive text and media attachments
  • No rider identification required

8Geo-Location

GPS capture with every check — plus geo-fencing and heat map visualization, same as Talabat.

  • GPS coordinates captured with each compliance check
  • Real-time monitoring of Captain locations
  • Geo-fence zones with heat map coverage view

9Quests

Ad-hoc task assignments outside regular compliance checks — same as Talabat's quest system.

  • Assign tasks outside of regular check flows
  • Track completion and results
  • Connected to shift calendar — shows if Captain is on quest

10Shifts and Targets

Full shift management with daily and weekly target tracking.

  • Manage shift timings for all Captains
  • Assign daily and weekly check targets
  • Track progress against targets in real time

11Zoning

Define city zones, assign Captains, and get alerts if they operate outside their zone. Heat map coverage included.

  • Define city zones on a map
  • Assign zones to specific Captains
  • Automated alerts if operating outside assigned zone
  • Heat map visualization of zone coverage

12Dashboard and Backend Enhancements

Full OMS and compliance app overhaul — faster, optimized, with advanced reporting.

  • Modify questionnaires from the backend
  • Dashboard showing best and worst performing 3PLs
  • Violation report: missing, unhygienic, safety issues
  • Accept/reject/modify checks with auditor accountability tracking
  • Reports: checks completed, equipment defects, compliance rates, actions/deductions, Captain performance
  • OMS and compliance app 2.0 — faster and fully optimized
Why free? These upgrades strengthen the compliance layer that HungerStation already uses. When combined with the 3PL Operations License (paid by 3PLs), the entire ecosystem becomes more valuable — better compliance data, connected gear records, and a complete picture of 3PL performance. HungerStation gets a significantly upgraded product at zero additional cost.

Product 1 of 3

Inventory OS — from spreadsheet to digital backbone

Every piece of gear tracked from warehouse shelf to rider back to clearance. Photo-confirmed, timestamped, defensible.

Rider onboarding in 40 seconds

Search the rider by ID. The app displays each eligible gear item one by one. Select sizes, confirm issuance, take a photo of the rider with all received items. The entire kit is digitally assigned.

If an item is out of stock, the app tracks the gap and unlocks the missing item exchange when stock arrives.

Photo-confirmed gear acceptance

Every gear handoff requires a confirmation photo and a digital checklist. The rider and the 3PL both have proof of what was distributed, when, and in what condition. This is the single artifact that settles deduction disputes.

Clearance — riders leave, gear comes back

When a rider's contract ends, the clearance process records every returned item. Returned gear is categorized: reusable (back to stock), disposed, or transferred to another rider. Missing items trigger automated deductions with photo evidence.

Stock management at a glance

Each 3PL gets a real-time view of their warehouse stock per SKU, per size. Weekly stock checks, correction requests that flow through OMS approval, and top-up ordering to the central warehouse.

Gear metrics dashboardTotal issued, onboarding vs replacement, cost savings
Deduction metricsPenalties by type, sliding scale, filterable by 3PL
Rider metricsRiders served, peak hours, period comparison
Product 2 of 3

Compliance auditor — field checks connected to gear data

HungerStation's Captains already use this app. With the 2.0 upgrade (delivered free), compliance checks now link directly to gear records, geo-locations, quests, shifts, and a full performance backend.

Three-category rider checks

Every field check covers Appearance (uniform, branding, ID), Hygiene (cleanliness, food safety), and Vehicle (condition, plates, safety equipment). Each question is scored, every answer timestamped, photo evidence captured.

With the 2.0 upgrade: configurable questionnaires, rider search by plate number, and equipment view showing what gear the rider actually has.

Captain performance scorecard

Every Captain is measured on a weighted scorecard: number of audits (monthly target), accuracy (rated by supervisor with accept/reject/modify tracking), rider survey scores, ad-hoc quest completion, and attendance via clock-in/out.

The dashboard surfaces the best and worst performing 3PLs, violation reports, and full compliance rate breakdowns.

Product 3 of 3

Rider Hub — the rider-facing layer

Riders book their own gear appointments, view their assigned equipment, report accidents, and check in when they arrive. No phone calls, no walk-ins, no chaos.

Self-service appointment booking

Rider enters their ID, selects the service they need (new gear, exchange, clearance, size swap), picks an available time slot, and confirms. The 3PL warehouse sees the booking in the Inventory App and can prepare in advance.

Riders can reschedule or cancel. No more riders showing up unannounced.

Rider loginRider ID + verification question
My gearRider sees their assigned items
"I am here" check-inNotifies warehouse on arrival
3PL experience

Every 3PL gets a restricted but powerful view of the OMS — scoped to their own riders. Full gear history, deduction transparency, clearance status, rider profiles.

OMS sectionWhat it showsWhy the 3PL cares

HungerStation view

The aggregated intelligence layer — free to HungerStation

When 700+ 3PLs operate on the same platform, HungerStation gets a dashboard it has never had: real-time visibility into gear operations, compliance performance, and 3PL quality across the entire KSA fleet.

  • Failed checks by category

Operational dashboards

  • Scheduled exports by email
Gear metrics — aggregatedTotal distribution, onboarding vs replacement
Deduction metrics — by 3PLFinancial impact with filterable breakdown
Rider profile — central recordGear, operations, photos — one source of truth
The flywheel effect: 3PLs pay 100 SAR/month → they onboard riders digitally → gear lifecycle data flows into the platform → HS gets aggregated visibility → HS uses data to score 3PLs → top-performing 3PLs get rewarded → more 3PLs adopt → data gets richer → HS gets more value — without paying a single riyal more.
Commercial

3PL Operations License — pricing

One price. One platform. HungerStation mandates adoption — every 3PL operates on the same standard.

3PL Operations License

Per 3PL / Monthly

100 SAR
per 3PL partner / month
  • Full Inventory OS access
  • Rider Hub booking system
  • Limited OMS portal (gear, deductions, riders, clearance)
  • Gear issuance, exchange, clearance with photo proof
  • Stock management tools
  • Deduction transparency and protection
  • Weekly automated reports
  • 2FA secured access
0 SAR
Cost to HungerStation
FREE
Compliance 2.0 upgrade
How the mandate works: HungerStation mandates that all 3PL partners use the Boolanga Operations License as a condition of their partnership agreement. Each 3PL pays their own license fee of 100 SAR/month. HungerStation receives the full Compliance App 2.0 upgrade and aggregated data dashboard at no cost.
For the 3PL: 100 SAR/month is less than the cost of a single disputed deduction. One prevented overcharge pays for an entire year of the license. And for the first time, the 3PL has legal-grade proof of every gear transaction.
Rollout

Implementation timeline

A phased rollout starting with 10-15 pilot 3PLs, validating the model, and scaling to the full fleet within 90 days.

PhaseTimelineActionsMilestone
1. AlignmentWeek 1-2HS and Boolanga align on 3PL mandate language, select 10-15 pilot 3PLs (largest fleets), configure market-specific gear items, begin Compliance 2.0 deploymentSigned agreement, pilot list confirmed
2. PilotWeek 3-6Onboard pilot 3PLs, train warehouse staff on Inventory App, activate Rider Hub, link compliance data, roll out Compliance 2.0 to Captains10-15 3PLs live, Compliance 2.0 active
3. ValidateWeek 7-8Review pilot data with HS — deduction accuracy, onboarding speed, compliance coverage. Adjust configuration. Collect 3PL feedback.Pilot KPIs hit, 3PL testimonials
4. ScaleWeek 9-12Roll out to full 700+ fleet in batches (100-150 per week). Automated onboarding. HS mandates adoption in 3PL agreements.Full fleet live, HS dashboard at scale
5. OptimizeOngoing3PL performance scoring live, quarterly business reviews, feature iteration based on HS prioritiesContinuous improvement cycle
